input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
    Title="MyMauiApp"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard — single page, shown as a flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products — flyout item with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings — separate flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.reasoningText:
The user wants a .NET MAUI AppShell.xaml setup with a specific structure. This is a knowledge/guidance question - I don't need to explore a codebase. Let me provide the XAML and code-behind directly.

Structure needed:
1. Flyout with "Dashboard" (single page)
2. "Products" with two tabs: "Active" and "Archived"
3. Settings page in flyout
4. Route registration for ProductDetailsPage

Let me create the files to demonstrate this.

            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

        <Tab Title="Active">

                Title="Active"

                Route="active" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent

        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings — separate flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="settings.png">
        <ShellContent

            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.content:
Here are the two files:

**`AppShell.xaml`** — defines the flyout structure:

- **Dashboard** → single `ShellContent` (no tabs).
- **Products** → two `<Tab>` children ("Active" / "Archived") rendered as bottom tabs when this flyout item is selected.
- **Settings** → its own flyout item.

**`AppShell.xaml.cs`** — registers the detail route:

`Routing.RegisterRoute("productdetails", ...)` makes it navigable via:

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync("productdetails", new Dictionary<string, object>
{
    { "Product", selectedProduct }
});
```

**Key points:**

| Concept | How it works |
|---|---|
| `FlyoutItem` | Top-level entry in the hamburger menu |
| `Tab` inside a `FlyoutItem` | Creates bottom tabs within that section |
| `ShellContent` | Wraps an actual page; `ContentTemplate` defers creation |
| `Routing.RegisterRoute` | Needed for pages **not** in the visual tree (like detail pages pushed onto the nav stack) |

To receive the navigation parameter in `ProductDetailsPage`, apply `[QueryProperty]` or implement `IQueryAttributable`.
